trigger: review caught missing mocks in 7 test files
insight: When adding new API client functions (like fetchSettings/updateSettings) to api-client.ts, ALL test files that vi.mock('@/lib/api-client') need updating with the new functions. In Pearl, 7+ integration/view test files mock api-client. Use 'grep -r vi.mock.*api-client' to find them all. Missing mocks cause runtime errors like 'No fetchSettings export is defined on the mock' that cascade across seemingly unrelated test files.

trigger: review caught PUT missing from CORS header
insight: Pearl's CORS header in server.ts must include ALL HTTP methods used by routes. When adding PUT /api/settings, the CORS Allow-Methods header needed 'PUT' added alongside GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE, OPTIONS. Without it, every browser preflight for PUT fails silently. Check CORS headers whenever adding a new HTTP method to any route.

trigger: review caught settings PUT blocked in embedded mode
insight: Pearl's embedded mode guard in server.ts blocks ALL non-GET /api/ routes except health/migration/setup. When adding routes that don't depend on Dolt (like /api/settings which reads/writes filesystem), they must be explicitly whitelisted in the guard. Otherwise they get a 503 EMBEDDED_DEPRECATED error despite being safe to run without Dolt.

trigger: review caught TOCTOU and non-atomic write
insight: For settings/config files written to disk, use atomic write pattern: write to a .tmp sibling file first, then rename (rename is atomic on POSIX). Also avoid the existsSync+mkdirSync TOCTOU race — just call mkdirSync with {recursive: true} unconditionally since it's a no-op when the directory exists. Both patterns were caught in review of saveSettings in settings-loader.ts.
